commit.message:
fix: MET Norway 48-hour window bug — filter todayHigh/todayLow to today only (Rec 3)

The old code used series.slice(0, 48) which grabbed 48 hours from the
current time, leaking tomorrow's peak temperatures into today's high.
During heat waves, this inflated MET Norway's todayHigh by several
degrees.

Now filters the timeseries to only entries matching today's local date
(midnight to midnight SAST), using utcOffsetSeconds for correct timezone
conversion.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: probably-weather-new-b
repository.description: weather app

commit.message:
feat: dynamic source weight adjustment for heat wave accuracy (Rec 1 & 2)

Detect when Open-Meteo and WeatherAPI report near-identical daily highs
(within 0.5°C), indicating they share the same ECMWF model — halve WA
weight to avoid double-counting the cold bias.

When MET Norway diverges >5°C above ECMWF-family average, boost MET
Norway weight to 40% and reduce Open-Meteo to 25% — MET Norway handles
SA heat waves more accurately.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Refactor: parking_lot migration, unwrap elimination, hot path optimization, DRY, dialog decomposition, red error status

- Migrate std::sync::Mutex to parking_lot::Mutex across 9 files (no poisoning, faster)
- Eliminate production .unwrap() calls with let-else and .expect() patterns
- Cache column headers and pre-compute file size/date strings to reduce per-frame allocations
- Remove UI-thread blocking path.exists() call in history dialog
- Unify duplicate format_bytes/format_size, extract clear_previews_except helper
- Show error status messages in red bold text via set_status/set_status_error methods
- Decompose 553-line show_dialogs() into 9 focused functions
- Add missing copy_file_or_dir (non-overwrite) to fix pre-existing test compilation errors
- Document refactoring workflow in CLAUDE.md

Rewrite START-HERE.md: locks first, hand earned

~190 lines, no names, direct tone.

commit.message:
feat: major battle screen animation overhaul

- Player lunges forward (x slide) when answering correctly, recoils on wrong
- Boss violent shake with multiple frames (not single translateX) on hit
- Boss white flash (brightness 8 + desaturate) on hit — classic Pokemon hit effect
- Boss continuous float idle animation (y bounce, 2.6s cycle) — always moving
- Player continuous float idle animation (y bounce, 2.2s cycle, different timing)
- Boss enraged: red drop-shadow glow
- Boss HP bar: glows red when below 25%, smooth width transition via framer-motion
- Screen flash overlay: green on correct, red on wrong (full-screen, 300ms)
- Damage numbers: framer-motion pop (scale 1→1.5) + float up, larger font
- Streak counter: bounces and grows on each new streak
- Timer: animated pulse when ≤10s
- Background: animated radial gradient that breathes + 12 floating particles
- Stage ground: gradient floor line giving sprites a surface to stand on
- Shadow ovals under each sprite for depth
- Boss glow: drop-shadow using themeColor in idle state
- Removed old CSS-class-based shake, all animation via framer-motion

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: resolve TypeScript narrowing error for Config.auth0 in auth.ts (#35)

Capture Config.auth0 into a local const after the null guard to preserve
TypeScript's type narrowing across async/await boundaries, fixing the
build error: 'Config.auth0' is possibly 'undefined'.

perf: optimize images, fix preloads, add robots.txt — ~5.9MB payload reduction

- Fix hero preloads: was preloading unused hero-lloret (3.3MB), now preloads hero-dive
- Optimize hero-dive-desktop: 1.3MB → 175KB (q85, 1920px)
- Optimize hero-dive-tablet: 357KB → 67KB (q85, 1024px)
- Optimize 13 boat images over 150KB (q82, 800px max)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Use dashboard.codexhelper.com instead of pages.dev for session sharing

The session_token cookie uses SameSite=Lax + Domain=.codexhelper.com,
which means it is only sent for same-site requests. The pages.dev domain
is a completely different site so the cookie was never included in API
calls, causing login to loop and sessions not to transfer. Using the
dashboard.codexhelper.com subdomain keeps everything same-site.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add unit tests to improve coverage and enhance test reliability (#48)

This pull request introduces new unit tests for several custom hooks and
utility functions, improving test coverage and reliability. The tests
cover `useCanvas`, `useMediaQuery`, `useKeyboardShortcuts`,
`useImageStore`, and analytics utilities, ensuring proper behavior and
cleanup. Additionally, a small change was made to the analytics
initialization logic to prevent unnecessary script injection when the
measurement ID is missing, and a bug was fixed where
`ImageBitmap.close()` was not reliably called due to React 19's deferred
setState updaters.

**New test coverage:**

* Added comprehensive tests for the `useCanvas` hook, including handling
of null refs, setup and teardown of `ResizeObserver`, and cleanup on
unmount. (`src/hooks/__tests__/useCanvas.test.ts`)
* Added tests for the `useMediaQuery` hook, verifying initial state,
updates on media query changes, and listener cleanup.
(`src/hooks/__tests__/useMediaQuery.test.ts`)
* Added tests for `useKeyboardShortcuts`, covering zoom, tool mode, grid
toggle, position lock, and modifier key handling.
(`src/hooks/__tests__/useKeyboardShortcuts.test.tsx`)
* Added tests for `useImageStore`, covering image load/replace/close,
viewport/tool/ROI/line-profile state, grid enable/disable, and bitmap
cleanup. (`src/hooks/__tests__/useImageStore.test.tsx`)
* Added tests for analytics utilities, including idempotent
initialization, script injection, and event tracking behavior.
(`src/utils/__tests__/analytics.test.ts`)

**Bug fixes:**

* Updated `initGA4` in `src/utils/analytics.ts` to prevent script
injection when the measurement ID is empty.
* Fixed `ImageBitmap` cleanup in `useImageStore` — the "stale" pattern
(reading `prev` state inside a `setState` updater and closing bitmaps
after `setState` returns) does not work in React 19 because updaters may
be deferred. Replaced with ref-based reads (`imageRef`, `gridStateRef`)
to reliably close old bitmaps before setting new state.

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai
-->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Prevented analytics from loading without a valid ID.
* More reliable image resource cleanup and steadier image/grid
transitions to reduce leaks and race conditions.

* **Accessibility**
* Improved keyboard interaction reliability by ensuring page focus
before key events.

* **Tests**
* Large suite of unit and end-to-end tests for hooks, keyboard
shortcuts, media queries, image/grid behaviors, and analytics.
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

commit.message:
fix: BYOK agents default connectionMethod to null, not WEBSOCKET

Agents without an AgentRegistration are BYOK agents that need the
Go Proxy trigger. The connectionMethod default of "WEBSOCKET" was
incorrectly routing them to the SDK skip path, preventing streaming.

- Web API: return null instead of "WEBSOCKET" when no registration
- Gateway: remove "WEBSOCKET" default from Map.get so absent key = nil

commit.message:
Simplify and isolate MFE Docker builds; fix peer dependency warnings

- Switch all frontend Dockerfiles from quay.io images to docker.io
  standard images (node:24-alpine, nginx:stable-alpine), removing the
  USER root and ulimit workarounds that were RHEL-image-specific
- Make each MFE Dockerfile fully self-contained: copy only its own
  package.json, install standalone (no workspace stubs), build in
  isolation — shell and MFEs are completely unaware of each other at
  build time
- Move vite-plugin-css-injected-by-js from root devDependencies into
  each MFE's own devDependencies (was only available via hoisting)
- Remove hardcoded /home fallback from IndexRedirect — shell no longer
  encodes knowledge of any specific MFE route
- Fix peer dependency warnings via packageExtensions in .yarnrc.yml:
  @module-federation/vite and @typescript-eslint/utils both fail to
  re-declare typescript as a peer, breaking downstream resolution chains
- Update Makefile k8s-pull to reference the images actually in use
- Update README: MFE independence design decision, per-MFE CSS plugin
  ownership, corrected "Adding a new MFE" step 3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
